messages.content:
What is the activity series of metals?
ďżĽ
A.
A list of metals arranged in order of atomic number
ďżĽ
B.
A list of metals arranged in order of electronegativity ďżĽ
C.
A list of metals arranged in order of reactivity
ďżĽ
D.
A list of metals arranged in order of oxidation state
C. A list of metals arranged in order of reactivity

Which of the following best describes a double-replacement reaction?
ďżĽ
A.
A lone element takes the place of a different element in a compound.
ďżĽ
B.
Atoms in one compound switch places with atoms in another compound.  ďżĽ
C.
Two elements join together to form a new compound.
ďżĽ
D.
One compound splits up, forming two or more elements or new compounds.
B. Atoms in one compound switch places with atoms in another compound.

What is formed in the reaction KI(aq) + AgNO3(aq) ďżĽ AgI(s) + KNO3(aq)?
ďżĽ
A.
A precipitate
ďżĽ
B.
A gas
ďżĽ
C.
Water
ďżĽ
D.
A mixture

Which of the following reactions demonstrates a synthesis reaction?
ďżĽ
A.
Zn + 2HCl ďżĽ ZnCl2 + H2
ďżĽ
B.
Mg + H2SO4 ďżĽ MgSO4 + H2
ďżĽ
C.
2NaCl ďżĽ 2Na + Cl2
ďżĽ
D.
C + O2 ďżĽ CO2

Which of the following is an example of a decomposition reaction?
ďżĽ
A.
H2 + Cl2 ďżĽ 2HCl
ďżĽ
B.
2NaCl ďżĽ 2Na + Cl2
ďżĽ
C.
CaCl2 + Na2CO3 ďżĽ CaCO3 + 2NaCl
ďżĽ
D.
Zn + 2HCl ďżĽ ZnCl2 + H2

Which of the following is an example of a decomposition reaction?
ďżĽ
A.
H2 + Cl2 ďżĽ 2HCl
ďżĽ
B.
2NaCl ďżĽ 2Na + Cl2
ďżĽ
C.
CaCl2 + Na2CO3 ďżĽ CaCO3 + 2NaCl
ďżĽ
D.
Zn + 2HCl ďżĽ ZnCl2 + H2

What happens in a single-replacement reaction?
ďżĽ
A.
One reactant breaks apart to form new compounds.
ďżĽ
B.
Two reactants combine to form one product.   What happens in a single-replacement reaction?  ďżĽ
C.
One reactant replaces an element in another reactant.
ďżĽ
D.
Two reactants exchange elements with each other.
C. One reactant replaces an element in another reactant.

What kind of reaction shows two reactants combining to form one new product?
ďżĽ
A.
A double-replacement reaction
ďżĽ
B.
A single-replacement reaction ďżĽ
C.
A decomposition reaction
ďżĽ
D.
A synthesis reaction

What does it mean if something is insoluble?
ďżĽ
A.
It will not dissolve in water.
ďżĽ
B.
It exists in a liquid state.
ďżĽ
C.
It releases a gas upon reacting.
ďżĽ
D.
It is not reactive.
A. It will not dissolve in water.
